requirements.desc: scan --dry-run outputs single-layer JSON of category-name to entry-count integers; analyzedAt and per-category structure shall be excluded
requirements.note: `src/docs/commands/scan.js` now outputs `{ [category]: count }` for `--dry-run` and does not emit full analysis fields like `analyzedAt`.

requirements.desc: --dry-run output shall include zero-count categories whose scan DataSource is registered
requirements.note: Dry-run summary is initialized from all registered `dataSources` with `0`, then overwritten with actual counts.
When `ctx.dryRun` is true (and `--stdout` is not), code only writes to stdout; file write branch is skipped.

requirements.desc: --stdout shall output the full analysis JSON (existing behavior preserved)
When --stdout and --dry-run are combined, --stdout takes precedence and full JSON is output
requirements.note: Output logic checks `ctx.stdout` first, then `ctx.dryRun`, so combined flags produce full analysis JSON.

requirements.desc: Other subcommands --dry-run behavior shall remain unchanged
requirements.note: Diff changes scan command only; no non-scan command implementation files were modified.

requirements.desc: Preset creation guide (ja/en) shall document summary form for --dry-run
requirements.note: Both ja/en preset guides were updated in templates and generated docs to describe summary JSON and zero-count category behavior.

requirements.desc: scan exit code contract: 0 on success, non-zero on error
requirements.note: Success-path exit code 0 is covered by new tests, but this diff does not explicitly verify or change non-zero behavior on error cases.

unplanned.change: New e2e contract test file added (test additions are not part of the 8 listed requirements).
Existing scan dry-run test was updated to new summary contract.

summary.notes: Core behavior requirements R1-R7 are implemented in code/docs as specified. R8 is mostly preserved, but error-path non-zero exit behavior is not directly evidenced by this diff.
